name: Oatmeal Brickle Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Grease baking sheets with shortening. Place sheets of foil on countertop for cooling cookies.
2. For the cookies, combine shortening, brown sugar, egg, milk, and vanilla in a large bowl. Beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until well blended.
3. Combine oats, flour, baking soda, and salt. Mix into the creamed mixture at low speed just until blended. Stir in almond Brickle chips.
4. Shape dough into 3/4-inch (2 cm) balls. Place 1 inch (2.5 cm) apart on prepared baking sheet.
5. Bake one baking sheet at a time at 375°F (190°C) for 8 to 10 minutes, or until lightly browned. Do not overbake. Cool 2 minutes on the baking sheet. Remove cookies to foil to cool completely.
6. For the icing, place white chocolate and shortening in a heavy resealable sandwich bag. Seal. Microwave at 50% (MEDIUM) power. Knead the bag after 1 minute. Repeat until smooth. Alternatively, melt by placing in a bowl of hot water. Cut a tiny tip off the corner of the bag. Pipe heart shapes on cookies or drizzle randomly.
Makes: About 8-1/2 dozen small cookies
